next_step_text: Small update as I am in Struer today and have talked to finance. As finance are swamped in the year end activities June/May, we have pushed next step to later in June.

recent_activities_14d.subject: Re-engage Jonas Elber directly within 7 days with a co-created business-case draft (quantified ROI model: contract cycle

our_open_deliverables.text: Deliver CLM and P2P commercial proposals to Jonas Elber within 48 hours
Send email and short video to Jonas outlining Outlook integration options for contract email capture (auto-link emails to contract records without scanning every inbox)
Share three customer reference videos with Nickolaj Almskou Holler
Provide ballpark pricing estimates for integration, licensing, and implementation to Nickolaj and finance team
Arrange sandbox or POC environment for hands-on evaluation by B&O team
Record and send demo video covering tax engine capabilities
Record additional demo sessions for contract management and long-tail spend modules and share with B&O team
Schedule December meeting with Casper and Anthony in Denmark (Copenhagen or Jutland) to finalize commercial terms and timeline ahead of IT and Digital Board presentation

buyer_dependent_items.text: Provide the user-count breakdown for CLM and P2P needed to finalize commercial pricing.
Schedule December meeting with Casper and Anthony in Denmark (Copenhagen or Jutland) to finalize commercial terms and timeline ahead of IT and Digital Board presentation
Schedule and hold IT and Digital Board presentation (final approval gate) with CFO, IT, and business leads to approve vendor selection and budget
Confirm centralized vs. decentralized P2P operating model to finalise user counts and Intake module sizing for pricing

competitive_position.summary: A formal ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation with two unnamed peer finalists, layered on incumbent displacement (SAP ECC/Bear Shop, SharePoint, Adobe Sign). The strongest live dynamic is the undecided ERP path (Dynamics-leaning), which makes dual-ERP support an evaluation lever; Zycus's defensible edge is AI maturity, which Jonas rated ahead of competition on 25 Nov 2025. The unresolved threat is a Dynamics-native or cheaper peer reaching the board with a stronger business case while our pricing is still outstanding.

competitors.name: Unnamed peer S2P vendors (2, in ITTStc 3-vendor evaluation)
competitors.quote: ITTStc approved we continue the P2P evaluation based on 3 vendors with a S2P focus. Vendor selection end of April and recommendation/approval... end of May/beg June
competitors.how_we_win: Get our standalone S2P pricing (Zycus-delivered, no partner) into Jonas's hands now so we are not the late vendor; force a named-competitor disclosure on the next call; defend the Merlin agentic-AI wedge with a quantified ROI model and sandbox before the board decision.

competitors.name: Microsoft Dynamics (future ERP path)
competitors.quote: I would be surprised if we don't go Microsoft way, but of course there needs to be a decision
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with proven dual-ERP support (auto-routing line items, separate POs per legal entity) so a Dynamics migration is a non-issue regardless of vendor; pre-empt a Dynamics-native P2P peer on AI maturity.

competitors.name: SAP ECC + Bear Shop (incumbent ERP/procurement)
competitors.quote: it has a lot of errors and limitations, and it cannot support dual ERP solutions; this application will be decommissioned next year
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus P2P to replace the failing Bear Shop free-text flow with catalog control and spend visibility; decommission timeline forces a 2026 decision.

competitors.name: SharePoint (incumbent CLM repository)
competitors.quote: 10,000 contracts stored in a SharePoint somewhere, and it was a little bit difficult to manage
competitors.how_we_win: AI bulk migration with metadata extraction and clause-based search displaces manual SharePoint management.

competitors.name: Adobe Sign (incumbent eSignature)
competitors.quote: We are using Adobe Sign today... does that mean we can remove our license with Adobe Sign and utilize your capabilities?
competitors.how_we_win: Show Certinal as a priced optional line so B&O can compare against retaining Adobe Sign; integrate either way to remove adoption friction.

champion_strength.summary: Jonas Elber is a strong, active champion who owns the CLM recommendation, drove the no-RFP direct evaluation, publicly rated Zycus ahead on AI, and was promoted to Head of Technology on 29 May 2026 while preparing the business case. The gap is a second senior advocate at the board/CFO level.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is named by role but never engaged. On 25 Nov 2025 Jonas Elber stated 'the recommendation that the team does need to go to an IT and Digital Board where our CFO is involved... making the final decision of each of these projects,' and the 17 Mar 2026 ITTStc cadence confirms the same board owns the approval. Neither the CFO nor any board member has appeared on a call, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified is false. Jonas owns the CLM recommendation and his 29 May promotion to Head of Technology raises his weight, but he does not have unilateral authority. Because this is a non-forecasted deal in band terms, the route to power is THROUGH the champion: ask Jonas to broker a short CFO/board pre-brief (pain, ROI, AI wedge, timeline) before the recommendation deck is finalized — do not cold-approach the CFO.

recommended_moves: Deliver the standalone S2P module pricing (Zycus-delivered implementation, no partner) and unbundled CLM pricing with Certinal as an optional line straight to Jonas, so he can convert his proposal into the board recommendation.
Build a quantified ROI model (free-text spend leakage, contract-cycle time, legal review hours, invoice-AI time saved) and hand it to Jonas to drop into his board business case.
Resolve the user-count input by getting Jonas to confirm the centralized vs decentralized P2P operating model, which gates accurate Intake/P2P pricing.
